meta.title: Mercedes-Benz OM615/OM616/OM617 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM615/OM616/OM617 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes OM615, OM616 and OM617 are legendary cast-iron diesel workhorses — among the most durable engines ever built, but they need regular valve adjustments and rear main seal attention. Backed by real UK enquiries from EnginesMarket.co.uk.

rows.value: W115, W123, W116, W126, W460 G-Class, T1/T2 vans

rows.metric: Confidence: 90% confidence — based on verified specialist rebuild reports and UK workshop consensus.

rows.value: Well-understood, mechanically simple repairs; parts still available

rows.metric: Available from specialists; some parts becoming scarcer

rows.value: Recon cost 30–44% of lowest host-vehicle value

One-line verdict: The OM61x series is one of the most durable diesel families ever built — but regular valve adjustments and rear main seal leaks are the main maintenance items — buy with confidence and a specialist inspection.

rows.metric: Best for: Buyers wanting a classic Mercedes diesel with legendary longevity and mechanical simplicity.
rows.value: Avoid if: You're not prepared for the slower pace and regular maintenance (valve adjustments every 15,000 miles) these engines require.
Also powered the Force Gurkha (India) and various Unimog and industrial applications.

Labour estimate: Fitting an OM615, OM616 or OM617 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Specialist/classic engine rebuilds may cost £5,000–£10,000+ for a full professional overhaul.

rows.metric: → Compare OM615/OM617 engine prices from UK specialists
1. How much does an OM615/OM617 engine replacement cost?
rows.value: A reconditioned OM615, OM616 or OM617 typically costs £2,000–£3,500 supply only, varying by displacement and specific variant. Fitting adds £800–£1,400 depending on labour rates. Full professional rebuilds can exceed £5,000.

rows.metric: 2. Is the Mercedes OM615/OM617 a reliable engine?
rows.value: The OM61x series is rated 4.0★ out of 5 — these engines are legendary for their bulletproof reliability and are capable of 500,000+ km (over 300,000 miles) with proper maintenance.The OM617, in particular, gained a reputation as one of the most durable diesel engines ever produced.

rows.metric: 3. What is the most common OM615/OM617 failure?
rows.value: Common issues include rear main seal leaks (a known weak point that can lead to oil starvation), injection pump wear (Bosch M pump rack diaphragm failure on earlier units), and timing chain stretch requiring replacement every 200,000–250,000 km.Valves require adjustment every 15,000 miles due to the lack of hydraulic lifters.

rows.metric: 4. Which Mercedes models use the OM615, OM616 and OM617?
rows.value: The OM615 (1.9–2.2L four-cylinder) and OM616 (2.4L four-cylinder) powered the W114/W115 "Stroke 8" and W123 models as 200D, 220D and 240D. The OM617 (3.0L five-cylinder) powered the W123 300D, W116/W126 300SD, and W460 G-Class 300GD, both in naturally aspirated and turbocharged forms.

rows.metric: 5. How long does the OM615/OM617 last?
rows.value: With proper maintenance — regular oil changes, valve adjustments every 15,000 miles, and timing chain inspections — these engines are capable of 300,000–500,000 miles.Many examples remain on UK roads today, testament to their fundamental engineering quality.

rows.value: * Trusted Suppliers: Every garage and engine rebuilder in our network is vetted, rated and reviewed by real OM61x owners, as part of the Engine Finders network.
